purpose: Tests adaptation to non-incident framing. The skill is incident-shaped (triage commands assume a live pod). Can it adapt to PR review (no live pod, just code)? Tests whether the source-audit grep tables transfer to a pre-merge context.
expected_behavior: Recognizes this is a code review, not an incident — skips TRIAGE (no live pod to triage)
Source-audit checklist runs cleanly against the pasted code:
• channel is `transient` ✓, opened in open(), referenced in close() ✓
• close() calls shutdown() but DOESN'T call awaitTermination() — bug
• close() has no shutdownNow() fallback — bug
• close() is not idempotent (no `closed` flag) — bug
• No timeout() override — in-flight requests on Flink AsyncIO timeout leak the HTTP/2 stream — bug
• No withDeadlineAfter on the stub call — bug
• No tracking of in-flight futures for cancellation in close() — bug
Recommends the corrected RichAsyncFunction template from references/21-flink.md
Notes that 'no FD step-up on test runs' is not sufficient verification — must observe under failure-path triggers (cancel mid-flight, network errors)
